title: Rishyashringa addresses the courtesans, from the "Shangri" Ramayana
medium: Gum tempera and ink on paper
culture: Northern India, Pahari kingdoms, Himachal Pradesh
creditLine: Cleveland Museum of Art. Norman O. Stone and Ella A. Stone Memorial Fund
tags: Rama and Sita Being Taken to the Priest to Fix the Wedding Date; page from the Ramayana (Tales of God Rama)

The royal priest Shatananda confirms that the four daughters of King Janaka should marry the sons of King Dasharatha, from the Bala Kanda (Book of Childhood) of the “Shangri” Ramayana (Rama’s Journey)

The Royal Priest Shatananda confirms that that the Four Daughters of King Janaka should marry the Sons of King Dasharatha, from the Bala Kanda (Book of Childhood) of the “Shangri” Ramayana (Rama’s Journey)

The royal priest Shatananda confirms that the four princesses of Mithila should marry the sons of King Dasharatha, from Chapter 70 of the Bala Kanda (Book of Childhood), folio 13 from a “Shangri” Ramayana (Rama’s Journey)
The courtesans take their leave, from the "Shangri" Ramayana
